tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: teammate_A asked if I can do their part since I'm better at it. I feel a bit pressured to say yes to keep the peace, even though I'm already tired.

desires: Keep the peace with teammate_A so they don't feel like I'm burning bridges, even though I know I can't say no.
Finish my own project part tonight before it feels like a betrayal to drop it for the extra work.
Get through this task block without anyone noticing how drained I actually am.
Make sure I look reliable tomorrow so my cohort doesn't think I'm unreliable.

goals.description: Accept teammate_A's request to do their part tonight, even though it means doing double the work and feeling resentful.
goals.rationale: Keeping the peace with teammate_A is my top value; refusing would feel like burning a bridge, which I can't afford. I'll swallow my own project deadline to maintain closeness.

goals.description: Finish both parts of the project (mine + theirs) by tonight so I don't look unreliable tomorrow to the cohort.
goals.rationale: I need to appear reliable to my close friend group, but since I'm already overcommitting on this task, I have to prioritize getting it done now rather than risking a conflict later.

goals.description: Hide how exhausted I am during this task block by forcing myself to focus for just an hour before collapsing into sleep.
goals.rationale: This is too draining to plan right now. I'll just power through until the immediate pressure passes; worrying about my stamina will only add stress.
